tools.description: Create a new GitHub issue for tracking bugs, feature requests, or tasks. Use this for actionable work items that need assignment, labeling, and status tracking. For reports, announcements, or status updates that don't require task tracking, use create_discussion instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be created. Labels [test-reliability] will be automatically added.

body.description: Detailed issue description in Markdown. Do NOT repeat the title as a heading since it already appears as the issue's h1. Include context, reproduction steps, or acceptance criteria as appropriate.

labels.description: Labels to categorize the issue (e.g., 'bug', 'enhancement'). Labels must exist in the repository.

parent.description: Parent issue number for creating sub-issues. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 42 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/42). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123', 'aw_Test123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run.

temporary_id.description: Unique temporary identifier for referencing this issue before it's created. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 8 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Use '#aw_ID' in body text to reference other issues by their temporary_id; these are replaced with actual issue numbers after creation.

title.description: Concise issue title summarizing the bug, feature, or task. The title appears as the main heading, so keep it brief and descriptive.

tools.name: create_issue

tools.description: Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

reason.description: Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).

tool.description: Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.

tools.name: missing_tool

tools.description: Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.

message.description: Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').

tools.name: noop

tools.description: Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

context.description: Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).

data_type.description: Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.

reason.description: Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).

tools.name: missing_data

tools.description: Get details for a commit from a GitHub repository

include_diff.description: Whether to include file diffs and stats in the response. Default is true.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

sha.description: Commit SHA, branch name, or tag name

tools.name: get_commit

tools.description: Get the contents of a file or directory from a GitHub repository

ref.description: Accepts optional git refs such as `refs/tags/{tag}`, `refs/heads/{branch}` or `refs/pull/{pr_number}/head`

sha.description: Accepts optional commit SHA. If specified, it will be used instead of ref

tools.name: get_file_contents

annotations.title: Get a specific label from a repository.

tools.description: Get a specific label from a repository.
tools.name: get_label

tools.description: Get the latest release in a GitHub repository
tools.name: get_latest_release

tools.description: Get details of the authenticated GitHub user. Use this when a request is about the user's own profile for GitHub. Or when information is missing to build other tool calls.
tools.name: get_me

annotations.title: Get a release by tag name

tools.description: Get a specific release by its tag name in a GitHub repository
tools.name: get_release_by_tag

tools.description: Get details about a specific git tag in a GitHub repository
tools.name: get_tag

tools.description: Get member usernames of a specific team in an organization. Limited to organizations accessible with current credentials

org.description: Organization login (owner) that contains the team.

tools.name: get_team_members

tools.description: Get details of the teams the user is a member of. Limited to organizations accessible with current credentials

user.description: Username to get teams for. If not provided, uses the authenticated user.

tools.name: get_teams

tools.description: Get information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository.

method.description:
The read operation to perform on a single issue.
Options are:
1. get - Get details of a specific issue.
2. get_comments - Get issue comments.
3. get_sub_issues - Get sub-issues of the issue.
4. get_labels - Get labels assigned to the issue.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: issue_read

tools.description: List branches in a GitHub repository

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: list_branches

tools.description: Get list of commits of a branch in a GitHub repository. Returns at least 30 results per page by default, but can return more if specified using the perPage parameter (up to 100).

author.description: Author username or email address to filter commits by

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

sha.description: Commit SHA, branch or tag name to list commits of. If not provided, uses the default branch of the repository. If a commit SHA is provided, will list commits up to that SHA.

tools.name: list_commits

tools.description: List supported issue types for repository owner (organization).

owner.description: The organization owner of the repository

tools.name: list_issue_types

tools.description: List issues in a GitHub repository. For pagination, use the 'endCursor' from the previous response's 'pageInfo' in the 'after' parameter.

after.description: Cursor for pagination. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.

direction.description: Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.

orderBy.description: Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

state.description: Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided

tools.name: list_issues

tools.description: List pull requests in a GitHub repository. If the user specifies an author, then DO NOT use this tool and use the search_pull_requests tool instead.

head.description: Filter by head user/org and branch

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: list_pull_requests

tools.description: List releases in a GitHub repository

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: list_releases

tools.description: List git tags in a GitHub repository

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: list_tags

annotations.title: Get details for a single pull request

tools.description: Get information on a specific pull request in GitHub repository.

method.description:
Action to specify what pull request data needs to be retrieved from GitHub. 
Possible options: 
 1. get - Get details of a specific pull request.
 2. get_diff - Get the diff of a pull request.
 3. get_status - Get status of a head commit in a pull request. This reflects status of builds and checks.
 4. get_files - Get the list of files changed in a pull request. Use with pagination parameters to control the number of results returned.
 5. get_review_comments - Get review threads on a pull request. Each thread contains logically grouped review comments made on the same code location during pull request reviews. Returns threads with metadata (isResolved, isOutdated, isCollapsed) and their associated comments. Use cursor-based pagination (perPage, after) to control results.
 6. get_reviews - Get the reviews on a pull request. When asked for review comments, use get_review_comments method.
 7. get_comments - Get comments on a pull request. Use this if user doesn't specifically want review comments. Use with pagination parameters to control the number of results returned.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: pull_request_read

tools.description: Fast and precise code search across ALL GitHub repositories using GitHub's native search engine. Best for finding exact symbols, functions, classes, or specific code patterns.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub's powerful code search syntax. Examples: 'content:Skill language:Java org:github', 'NOT is:archived language:Python OR language:go', 'repo:github/github-mcp-server'. Supports exact matching, language filters, path filters, and more.

tools.name: search_code

tools.description: Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub issues search syntax

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_issues

tools.description: Search for pull requests in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:pr

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only pull requests for this repository are listed.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub pull request search syntax

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only pull requests for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_pull_requests

tools.description: Find GitHub repositories by name, description, readme, topics, or other metadata. Perfect for discovering projects, finding examples, or locating specific repositories across GitHub.

minimal_output.description: Return minimal repository information (default: true). When false, returns full GitHub API repository objects.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Repository search query. Examples: 'machine learning in:name stars:>1000 language:python', 'topic:react', 'user:facebook'. Supports advanced search syntax for precise filtering.

sort.description: Sort repositories by field, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_repositories

[{"sha":"[REDACTED]","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-js/commit/[REDACTED]","commit":{"message":"Bump emitter version (#37583)

Updates TypeSpec emitter dependencies to the latest compatible versions
and regenerates the lock file.

### Packages impacted by this PR
`eng/emitter-package.json`, `eng/emitter-package-lock.json`,
`.scripts/automation_generate.sh`

### Issues associated with this PR


### Describe the problem that is addressed by this PR
Emitter dependencies were pinned to outdated versions. Upgrades are
limited by peer dependency constraints in
`@azure-tools/typespec-ts@0.50.3` (requires
`@azure-tools/typespec-azure-core@^0.65.0`, `@typespec/rest@^0.79.0`,
etc.), so only compatible packages were bumped:

- `@azure-tools/typespec-ts`: `0.50.2` → `0.50.3`
- `@typespec/compiler`: `1.9.0` → `1.10.0`
- `@autorest/typescript` range in `automation_generate.sh`: `^6.0.61` →
`^6.0.62`

Lock file regenerated via `tsp-client generate-lock-file`.

### What are the possible designs available to address the problem? If
there are more than one possible design, why was the one in this PR
chosen?
No design choice — straightforward version bump to latest
semver-compatible releases.

### Are there test cases added in this PR? _(If not, why?)_
No — dependency version updates to build tooling; no functional code
changed.

### Provide a list of related PRs _(if any)_
N/A

### Command used to generate this PR:**_(Applicable only to SDK release
request PRs)_
```
tsp-client generate-lock-file
```

### Checklists
- [ ] Added impacted package name to the issue description
- [ ] Does this PR needs any fixes in the SDK Generator?** _(If so,
create an Issue in the
[Autorest/typescript](https://github.com/Azure/autorest.typescript)
repository and link it here)_
- [ ] Added a changelog (if necessary)

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

> 
> ----
> 
> *This section details on the original issue you should resolve*
> 
> <issue_title>Bump emitter version</issue_title>
> <issue_description>1. Upgrade dependencies to the latest version in
`eng/emitter-package.json`.
> 2. run command `tsp-client generate-lock-file` to update
`emitter-package-lock.json`.
> 3. including `.scripts/automation_generate.sh`</issue_description>
> 
> ## Comments on the Issue (you are @copilot in this section)
> 
> <comments>
> </comments>
> 


</details>

- Fixes Azure/azure-sdk-for-js#37582

<!-- START COPILOT CODING AGENT TIPS -->

Adds `@danieljurek` and `@weshaggard` as CODEOWNERS for
`/eng/scripts/docs/type2docfx.version.txt`.

### Packages impacted by this PR

None — configuration change only.

### Issues associated with this PR

N/A

### Describe the problem that is addressed by this PR

`eng/scripts/docs/type2docfx.version.txt` had no CODEOWNERS entry,
leaving it without designated reviewers for changes to that file.

### What are the possible designs available to address the problem? If
there are more than one possible design, why was the one in this PR
chosen?

Single option: add the entry to `.github/CODEOWNERS` following existing
patterns in that file.

### Are there test cases added in this PR? _(If not, why?)_

No — CODEOWNERS is a GitHub configuration file with no associated test
infrastructure.

### Provide a list of related PRs _(if any)_

N/A

- [ ] Does this PR need any fixes in the SDK Generator?** _(If so,
create an Issue in the
[Autorest/typescript](https://github.com/Azure/autorest.typescript)
repository and link it here.)_

Co-authored-by: danieljurek <2158838+danieljurek@users.noreply.github.com>","author":{"name":"Copilot","email":"198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com","date":"2026-03-10T18:41:24Z"},"committer":{"name":"GitHub","email":"noreply@github.com","date":"2026-03-10T18:41:24Z"}},"author":{"login":"Copilot","id":198982749,"profile_url":"https://github.com/apps/copilot-swe-agent","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/1143301?v=4"},"committer":{"login":"web-flow","id":19864447,"profile_url":"https://github.com/web-flow","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/19864447?v=4"}},{"sha":"[REDACTED]","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-js/commit/[REDACTED]","commit":{"message":"[Monitor OpenTelemetry Exporter] Add GenAI Properties to be Exempt from Truncation (#37547)

### Packages impacted by this PR
@azure/monitor-opentelemetry-exporter

### Describe the problem that is addressed by this PR
This pull request refactors how the 64KB size limit for custom
dimensions is managed, especially for Gen AI scenarios. Instead of using
the environment variable `AZURE_MONITOR_DISABLE_CUSTOM_DIMENSIONS_LIMIT`
to disable truncation globally, it now exempts a specific set of Gen AI
property keys from truncation. The tests have been updated accordingly
to reflect this more targeted exemption.

**Custom Dimensions Truncation Policy:**

- Replaces the environment variable-based opt-out
(`ENV_AZURE_MONITOR_DISABLE_CUSTOM_DIMENSIONS_LIMIT`) with a hardcoded
set of exempt property keys (`CUSTOM_DIMENSIONS_EXEMPT_KEYS`) that are
allowed to exceed the 64KB limit, specifically for Gen AI payloads.
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-[REDACTED]L145-R157)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-[REDACTED]L44-R44)
[[3]](diffhunk://#diff-[REDACTED]L306-R307)
[[4]](diffhunk://#diff-[REDACTED]L324-R323)

**Testing Updates:**

- Removes all test logic and cases related to the environment variable,
and adds/updates tests to ensure only the specified exempt keys are not
truncated, while all others (including non-exempt Gen AI keys) are still
subject to the 64KB limit.
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-[REDACTED]L5-L24)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-[REDACTED]L94-R125)

### What are the possible designs available to address the problem? If
there are more than one possible design, why was the one in this PR
chosen?

### Checklists
- [x] Added impacted package name to the issue description
- [ ] Does this PR needs any fixes in the SDK Generator?** _(If so,
create an Issue in the
[Autorest/typescript](https://github.com/Azure/autorest.typescript)
repository and link it here)_
- [x] Added a changelog (if necessary)","author":{"name":"Jackson Weber","email":"47067795+JacksonWeber@users.noreply.github.com","date":"2026-03-10T17:26:38Z"},"committer":{"name":"GitHub","email":"noreply@github.com","date":"2026-03-10T17:26:38Z"}},"author":{"login":"JacksonWeber","id":47067795,"profile_url":"https://github.com/JacksonWeber","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/47067795?v=4"},"committer":{"login":"web-flow","id":19864447,"profile_url":"https://github.com/web-flow","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/19864447?v=4"}},{"sha":"[REDACTED]","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-js/commit/[REDACTED]","commit":{"message":"[search] Restore missing KnownEntityCategory, KnownEntityRecognitionSkillLanguage, KnownSentimentSkillLanguage exports (#37552)

## Describe the problem that is addressed by this PR

#37546 removed `backcompatTypes.ts` since these types are now generated
from TypeSpec, but we forgot to re-export them from the customization
layer. This meant `KnownEntityCategory`,
`KnownEntityRecognitionSkillLanguage`, and `KnownSentimentSkillLanguage`
were no longer available to consumers of the library.

This PR adds the enum and type alias definitions directly in the `src/`
customization layer (matching the generated code in content and position
to keep future `customization apply` merges clean) and re-exports them
through the public API.

API report now shows all three enums and their associated type aliases
again","author":{"name":"Maor Leger","email":"maorleger@users.noreply.github.com","date":"2026-03-10T16:58:09Z"},"committer":{"name":"GitHub","email":"noreply@github.com","date":"2026-03-10T16:58:09Z"}},"author":{"login":"maorleger","id":753570,"profile_url":"https://github.com/maorleger","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/753570?v=4"},"committer":{"login":"web-flow","id":19864447,"profile_url":"https://github.com/web-flow","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/19864447?v=4"}}]

arguments.body:
## Summary

CI check runs on commit [`6687e2f`](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-js/commit/[REDACTED]) (HEAD of `main` as of 2026-03-11) show failures across 4 packages and 3 build pipelines. The most actionable failure is **1 new** live-test failure in `@azure/storage-blob` from the recently merged CRC64 checksum feature (PR #37208), surfacing a potential bug in the structured message encoding. The remaining failures are either recurring live-test issues caused by external Azure service outages, or CI pipeline infrastructure errors (HTTP 503 from Azure Pipelines agent).

---

## Failures

### @azure/storage-blob — `js - storage-blob - tests`

**Check-run IDs:** 66596167217 (aggregate), 66594829356 (ubuntu node)

**Test counts:** 10 failed (1 new, 9 recurring) / 2,795 passed / 309 other / 3,114 total

**Failing tests (new):**
- `ContentChecksumValidation with client config - CRC64 > uploadStream should update progress event` in `sdk/storage/storage-blob/test/node/contentchecksum.spec.ts` (added in #37208)

**Error:**
```
The Structured Message was not valid.
RequestId:892e6d92-901e-0005-3235-b19f1f000000
Time:2026-03-11T08:58:50.6698569Z
```

**Root cause:**
This test runs only in live mode and was added as part of PR #37208 (CRC64 structured-message checksum for Storage). The Azure Storage service is rejecting the structured message with "not valid". Investigation of `sdk/storage/storage-common/src/StructuredMessageEncoding.ts` reveals two suspicious code issues:

1. **`fillInt64` copies only 6 bytes instead of 8 (Node.js path)**  
   Line 186: `for (let index = 0; index < 6; ++index)` — the loop should iterate 8 times to write a full 64-bit integer. Currently it leaves bytes 6–7 of the target buffer at their zero-initialized value, which is harmless for sizes < 2^48 but is still a latent bug.

2. **Message header stores `messageLength` (total, with framing) where the comment says "content length"**  
   Line 108:
   ```ts
   this.fillInt64(this.messageHeaderBuffer, 1, this.messageLength); // content length
   ```
   `this.messageLength` is computed as:
   ```ts
   this.messageLength =
     this.contentLength +
     MESSAGE_HEADER_LENGTH +
     (SEGMENT_HEADER_LENGTH + FOOTER_LENGTH) * this.segmentsCount +
     FOOTER_LENGTH;
   ```
   This is the **total framed message length** (for a 4 MB block: `4,194,304 + 39 = 4,194,343`), whereas the comment, and likely the service's expectation, is for the **original unframed content length** (`4,194,304`). The service also receives `x-ms-structured-content-length: <contentLength>` as a header, so a mismatch between that and the value embedded in the binary message header could explain the rejection.

**Suggested fix:**

For the `fillInt64` loop length:

```diff
--- a/sdk/storage/storage-common/src/StructuredMessageEncoding.ts
+++ b/sdk/storage/storage-common/src/StructuredMessageEncoding.ts
@@ -183,7 +183,7 @@
       const internalBuffer = Buffer.alloc(8);
       internalBuffer.writeBigUInt64LE(BigInt(input));

-      for (let index = 0; index < 6; ++index) {
+      for (let index = 0; index < 8; ++index) {
         buffer[offset + index] = internalBuffer[index];
       }
```

For the message-header content-length field, if the spec requires the unframed content length:

```diff
--- a/sdk/storage/storage-common/src/StructuredMessageEncoding.ts
+++ b/sdk/storage/storage-common/src/StructuredMessageEncoding.ts
@@ -106,7 +106,7 @@
   private handlingMessageHeader() {
     this.messageHeaderBuffer[0] = MESSAGE_VERSION;

-    this.fillInt64(this.messageHeaderBuffer, 1, this.messageLength); // content length
+    this.fillInt64(this.messageHeaderBuffer, 1, this.contentLength); // content length
```

> **Note:** Before applying the second fix, the structured message format spec should be confirmed (e.g. with the Azure Storage team) to verify whether offset 1–8 of the stream header is supposed to carry the original content length or the full encoded message length.

**Reproduced locally:** Not attempted — test requires `TEST_MODE=live` with live Azure Storage credentials.

---

### @azure/digital-twins-core — `js - digital-twins-core - tests`

**Check-run IDs:** 66599073930 (aggregate), 66598833779 (browser)

**Test counts:** 1 failed (1 new) / 517 passed / 7 other / 525 total

**Failing test:**
- `DigitalTwins - create, read, update, delete and telemetry operations > query digital twin` in `sdk/digitaltwins/digital-twins-core/test/` (live test)

**Error:**
```
Twin DTRelationshipsTestsFloorTwin has active relationships.
Cannot delete a twin unless all the relationships are deleted.
Please ensure that the active relationships on the twin are deleted.
See section on retrieving relationships https://aka.ms/adtv2twins.
```

**Root cause:**
This is a live-test environment issue. A previous test run left behind a twin (`DTRelationshipsTestsFloorTwin`) with active relationships in the Azure Digital Twins live instance, preventing the current run's cleanup/setup from deleting it. This is an orphaned-resource problem, not a code regression.

**Suggested fix:**
No code change needed. The live test environment should be cleaned up (delete orphaned twins/relationships). The test framework could also be hardened to tolerate pre-existing relationships by explicitly deleting relationships before deleting twins in `beforeEach`/`afterEach` hooks.

**Reproduced locally:** No (requires live Azure Digital Twins service).

---

### @azure/communication-identity — `js - communication-identity - tests`

**Check-run IDs:** 66596918581 (aggregate), 66596909408, 66596782728, 66596635512, 66596235247, 66596143649, 66596034022

**Test counts:** 12 failed (0 new, **12 recurring**) / 509 passed / 120 other / 641 total

**Failing tests (recurring):**
- `Get Token For Teams User [Playback/Live] [AAD]` (multiple runs)
- `Get Token For Teams User [Playback/Live]` (multiple runs)

**Error:**
```
invalid_grant: AADSTS5000224: We are sorry, this resource is not available.
If you are seeing this message by mistake, please contact Microsoft support.
Trace ID: 03a42967-b7ea-4d8b-b595-d1b3e8b80900
```

**Root cause:**
Azure Active Directory is returning `AADSTS5000224` (resource unavailable) for the Teams-user token grant. All 12 failures are **recurring** (pre-existing) and cross all test platforms (macOS, Windows, Ubuntu). This is a service-side AAD issue with the live test credential / tenant configuration — not a code regression.

**Suggested fix:**
No code change required. The CI live-test credential for Teams user tokens should be refreshed or the service issue should be investigated with the Azure AD team.

**Reproduced locally:** No (requires live AAD credentials for the specific tenant).

---

### @azure/communication-short-codes — `js - communication-short-codes - tests`

**Check-run IDs:** 66594813221 (aggregate), and 6 platform-specific runs

**Test counts:** 7 failed (0 new, **7 recurring**) / 134 passed / 1 other / 142 total

**Failing test (recurring):**
- `ShortCodesClient - manage Attachments > can manage Attachments` (across all platforms)

**Error:**
```
The server encountered an internal error.
```

**Root cause:**
The Azure Communication Services short-codes backend is returning HTTP 500 for attachment management operations. All 7 failures are **recurring**, appearing across all platforms, indicating a persistent service-side issue rather than a code regression.

**Suggested fix:**
No code change required. The Azure Communication Services short-codes service health should be investigated, or the live test environment credentials / subscription should be re-provisioned.

**Reproduced locally:** No (requires live Azure Communication Services credentials).

---

## Build / Pipeline Failures (non-test)

The following pipelines reported failures where **all unit tests passed** but a CI pipeline step failed. These are infrastructure issues and do not reflect test regressions:

| Check run | Failure type |
|-----------|--------------|
| `js - core` (66590410077) | Script exited with code 1 at build log line 116; 8,251 tests passed |
| `js - dataprotection - mgmt` (66600442371) | Script exited with code 1 at build log line 86; 16 tests passed |
| `js - appconfiguration - mgmt` (66593991920) | HTTP 503 from Azure DevOps agent service (`RestClient` fetch failure); 4 tests passed |

---

## Additional Notes

- The **storage-blob** failure is the only **new** test regression this cycle and is directly linked to the recently merged CRC64 structured-message checksum feature (PR #37208 landed 2026-03-06).
- All **communication-identity** and **communication-short-codes** failures are long-running recurring issues unrelated to recent code changes.
- The build-step failures (`js - core`, `js - dataprotection - mgmt`, `js - appconfiguration - mgmt`) do not affect test results and are likely transient CI infrastructure issues.
- Fixes for the `StructuredMessageEncoding.ts` issues should be paired with a new unit test (offline, no live service required) that validates the byte layout of an encoded structured message header against a known reference buffer.
arguments.title: CI test failures on main: @azure/storage-blob (new), @azure/communication-identity, @azure/digital-twins-core, @azure/communication-short-codes
